title: Docs are unusably slow
excerpt:
It takes 15-20 seconds for us to do anything on workdocs, even just hitting enter to start a new line in a bullet list. Internet speed is 108.4 Mbps download, 11.8 Mbps upload using Chrome. 
We spent a ton of time buildi…

posts.author: Kara4999 (Kara)
posts.cookedHtml:
It takes 15-20 seconds for us to do anything on workdocs, even just hitting enter to start a new line in a bullet list. Internet speed is 108.4 Mbps download, 11.8 Mbps upload using Chrome.

We spent a ton of time building out our system to manage status docs within Monday for our clients and it will be a significant lift to move them back out from Monday into Google Docs so I’m really hoping for a fix, but this is killing our workflow.

For reference we have text only files, no integrations into our doc or images, gifs etc.

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
While I don’t know if it will fix it, have you tried ensuring that chrome extensions are all turned off? Most extensions have to reprocess the page whenever there is a change. If you have many extensions, those could be slow downs in the browser.

This is because the way Docs work, pressing enter creates new structures within the page that extensions my process.

Not ideal but may help you identify if one extension is causing significant issues. Many extensions also have configuration to exclude sites, so it may be possible to turn them off on monday.com, esp. if they aren’t providing any benefit.

I’m only mentioning this because I am not experiencing anything like what you are. Everything is instant for me. Although, my internet is 900/250. The way docs works, the sending of updates to the backend should not be affecting the real-time editing that is why I’m wondering about extensions.

posts.author: Admeirel (Adolpho Meirelles)
posts.cookedHtml:
Having the same issue around here.
We rely on workdocs to take notes on really fast paced meetings.

Any news on this? @CXS

posts.author: TFaust (Timo Faust)
posts.cookedHtml: Same issues here. With every added doc it gets slower and laggier.

posts.author: Admeirel (Adolpho Meirelles)
posts.cookedHtml:
Guess it’s something that happens for time to time. My issue in November ended the same day or the next, and yesterday it was a bit sluggish for me as well.

Could be server related?
@Julietteb @BiancaT any info on this?

posts.author: C.Skinner (Chance Skinner)
posts.cookedHtml: One of my users is having a very similar problem. I notice a bit of slowness/delay when I work in Monday Docs but it is useable. His is easily 10-15 seconds to recognize any character input and often just doesn’t register his keystrokes. Anyone found a solution to this?

posts.author: Donaldcjapi (Donald)
posts.cookedHtml: I have the same problem, did you solve it?

posts.author: slanier (Sam Lanier)
posts.cookedHtml: any updates on this that anyone has seen? We are having the same issue and it is rather annoying.

posts.author: Jessica1 (Jessica Woitalla)
posts.cookedHtml: Same issue, is the a known solution yet?

posts.author: Lumexx (Marco Medina)
posts.cookedHtml: Same issue here in 2025 any updates on this?

posts.author: CarlosVeliz (Carlos)
posts.cookedHtml: The same issue with me and my team!

posts.author: katie_e (Katie Edwards)
posts.cookedHtml: This is something my team are facing too. At worst, it has taken up to 4 minutes for a change to be saved in docs (mostly just text boxes). It would be great to get some word from Monday.com on how to reduce this.

posts.author: LaLich (Lauren Lichterman)
posts.cookedHtml: I spoke to someone in customer service last week and was told that it is a known issue that docs start to slow down once they reach 1000 “blocks” which are just lines in the document. to know how many blocks/lines you have easily, CTRL + A to select all then see the number in parentheses next to “add new item.” My team is going to switch to quarterly notes documents for some of our clients to help with this, though that’s really really frustrating. hopefully they can solve this in the future! it also doesn’t seem to be exclusively an issue with docs that have that many blocks. it’s a problem with multiple people in at the same time too.

posts.author: Julia.Haupt (Julia Haupt)
posts.cookedHtml: Did they say whether we can expect this issue to improve or be fixed in the future? Or should we assume that this will always be the case?

posts.author: LaLich (Lauren Lichterman)
posts.cookedHtml: sounds like they are working to improve it. i didn’t get a timeline though.

posts.author: popjaco (Poppy)
posts.cookedHtml: We’ve build all our client reporting in docs, to summarise multiple boards in one place, but they’re so slow to load that the clients won’t use them, and often can’t. When we embed a board, the far right columns just won’t load and so they’re unusable.

posts.author: james-unrvld (James Carrington)
posts.cookedHtml: March 2026 here . I’m trying to move our MS Loop pages into Monday docs so that we have everything in one place; however, I am seriously struggling. I’m on quite a powerful MacBook Pro using Chrome browser, and I literally have to wait like 5 to 10 seconds between each action, even when just trying to select a paragraph of text and delete it. There’s nothing much on my page; it’s simply like an agenda, like a one-page agenda. It’s crazy slow. I don’t know if it’s a problem with my set-up or if it’s genuinely just a bug of these Monday Docs.
